boundaries: Scheduler sits on top of the agent runtime instead of the reverse direction.
Console code should go through scheduler and agent facades rather than reading scheduler store internals directly.
Public agent-facing types should enter through the correct facade instead of internal runtime modules when called from outside the agent package.
The public docs site is static and separate from the internal Console web app.

console_layout.label: Control plane
console_layout.responsibility: FastAPI control plane and runtime integration layer.

console_layout.label: API boundary
console_layout.responsibility: HTTP and SSE API boundary for requests and responses.

console_layout.label: Application services
console_layout.responsibility: Application services for runtime management, registry, tool catalog, session storage, and metrics.

console_layout.label: Shared models
console_layout.responsibility: Console-facing shared runtime, configuration, and view models.

console_layout.label: Channel adapters
console_layout.responsibility: Channel adapters for delivery, parsing, and integration workflows.

console_layout.label: Internal web UI
console_layout.responsibility: Internal control-plane frontend for sessions, traces, scheduler, and settings.

runtime_surfaces.role: Public entry for the canonical agent runtime, agent configuration, execution handles, and related types.
Public entry for orchestration, persistent roots, routing, waiting, and scheduler-backed agent coordination.
Public entry for tool contracts, tool results, execution context, and builtin tool integration.
Public entry for model abstractions, provider implementations, and model construction helpers.

sdk_layout.label: Agent runtime
sdk_layout.responsibility: Canonical agent runtime, execution loop, runtime state, models, nested-agent adapters, and persistence hooks.

sdk_layout.label: Model layer
sdk_layout.responsibility: Model abstraction, provider adapters, configuration policy, and factory construction.

sdk_layout.label: Tool layer
sdk_layout.responsibility: Tool contracts, builtin tools, execution context, process registry, and tool-side persistence.

sdk_layout.label: Scheduler
sdk_layout.responsibility: Agent-level orchestration, runtime tools, lifecycle management, and scheduler state persistence.

sdk_layout.label: Observability
sdk_layout.responsibility: Trace and span storage, querying, and runtime trace adaptation.

sdk_layout.label: Skills
sdk_layout.responsibility: Skill discovery, allowlisting, loading, and skill-to-tool bridging.

sdk_layout.label: Workspace
sdk_layout.responsibility: Workspace path semantics, bootstrap, and runtime workspace helpers.

sdk_layout.label: Memory
sdk_layout.responsibility: Shared workspace memory indexing, chunking, and search services.

sdk_layout.label: Configuration
sdk_layout.responsibility: Global SDK configuration and shared provider settings.

sdk_layout.label: Shared utilities
sdk_layout.responsibility: Cross-module runtime utilities and shared storage support.

summary: Agiwo is organized around a canonical agent runtime, a separate scheduler orchestration layer, a tool abstraction, a model layer, and an internal control plane.

supporting_layout.label: SDK tests
supporting_layout.responsibility: SDK test suite organized by subsystem.

supporting_layout.label: Repo scripts
supporting_layout.responsibility: Lint entrypoints, repo guardrails, and maintenance helpers.

supporting_layout.label: Repository docs
supporting_layout.responsibility: Repository-native design notes, concepts, and architecture documentation.

supporting_layout.responsibility: Template content consumed by runtime features.
